defs: From a higher place to a lower; in a descending course; as, to tend, move, roll, look, or take root, downward or downwards.
From a higher to a lower condition; toward misery, humility, disgrace, or ruin.
quotes: And downward fell into a groveling swine.

defs: From a remote time; from an ancestor or predecessor; from one to another in a descending line.
quotes: A ring the county wears, That downward hath descended in his house, From son to son, some four or five descents.
